name: red-discordbot

metadata.name: Red-DiscordBot
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)

documentation:
Red Discord Bot

Music, Moderation, Trivia, Stream Alerts and Fully Modular.

Overview • Installation • Documentation • Plugins • Community • License

# Overview Red is a fully modular bot – meaning all features and commands can be enabled/disabled to your liking, making it completely customizable. This is a *self-hosted bot* – meaning you will need to host and maintain your own instance. You can turn Red into an admin bot, music bot, trivia bot, new best friend or all of these together! [Installation](#installation) is easy, and you do **NOT** need to know anything about coding! Aside from installing and updating, every part of the bot can be controlled from within Discord. **The default set of modules includes and is not limited to:** - Moderation features (kick/ban/softban/hackban, mod-log, filter, chat cleanup) - Trivia (lists are included and can be easily added) - Music features (YouTube, SoundCloud, local files, playlists, queues) - Stream alerts (Twitch, Youtube, Picarto) - Bank (slot machine, user credits) - Custom commands - Imgur/gif search - Admin automation (self-role assignment, cross-server announcements, mod-mail reports) - Customisable command permissions **Additionally, other [plugins](#plugins) (cogs) can be easily found and added from our growing community of cog repositories.** # Installation **The following platforms are officially supported:** - [Windows](https://docs.discord.red/en/stable/install_guides/windows.html) - [MacOS](https://docs.discord.red/en/stable/install_guides/mac.html) - [Most major linux distributions](https://docs.discord.red/en/stable/install_guides/index.html) If after reading the guide you are still experiencing issues, feel free to join the [Official Discord Server](https://discord.gg/red) and ask in the **#support** channel for help. # Plugins Red is fully modular, allowing you to load and unload plugins of your choice, and install 3rd party plugins directly from Discord! A few examples are: - Cleverbot integration (talk to Red and she talks back) - Ban sync - Welcome messages - Casino - Reaction roles - Slow Mode - AniList - And much, much more! Feel free to take a [peek](https://index.discord.red) at a list of available 3rd party cogs! # Join the community! **Red** is in continuous development, and it’s supported by an active community which produces new content (cogs/plugins) for everyone to enjoy. New features are constantly added. If you can’t [find](https://index.discord.red) the cog you’re looking for, consult our [guide](https://docs.discord.red/en/stable/guide_cog_creation.html) on building your own cogs! Join us on our [Official Discord Server](https://discord.gg/red)! # License Released under the [GNU GPL v3](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.en.html) license. Red is named after the main character of "Transistor", a video game by [Super Giant Games](https://www.supergiantgames.com/games/transistor/). Artwork created by [Sinlaire](https://sinlaire.deviantart.com/) on Deviant Art for the Red Discord Bot Project. This project vendors [discord.ext.menus](https://github.com/Rapptz/discord-ext-menus) package made by Danny Y. (Rapptz) which is distributed under MIT License. A copy of this license can be found in the [discord-ext-menus.LICENSE](redbot/vendored/discord-ext-menus.LICENSE) file in the [redbot/vendored](redbot/vendored) folder of this repository.

modules.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session
documentation: Module to manage trivia sessions.

functions.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session._parse_answers
documentation:
Parse the raw answers to readable strings.

The reason this exists is because of YAML's ambiguous syntax. For example,
if the answer to a question in YAML is `yes`, YAML will load it as the
boolean value `True`, which is not necessarily the desired answer. This
function aims to undo that for bools, and possibly for numbers in the
future too.

# Parameters

answers

: The raw answers loaded from YAML.

# Returns

`tuple` of `str`

: The answers in readable/ guessable strings.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.start
documentation:
Create and start a trivia session.

This allows the session to manage the running and cancellation of its
own tasks.

Parameters
----------
ctx : `commands.Context`
    Same as `TriviaSession.ctx`
question_list : `dict`
    Same as `TriviaSession.question_list`
settings : `dict`
    Same as `TriviaSession.settings`

Returns
-------
TriviaSession
    The new trivia session being run.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession._error_handler
documentation: Catches errors in the session task.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.run
documentation:
Run the trivia session.

In order for the trivia session to be stopped correctly, this should
only be called internally by `TriviaSession.start`.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession._iter_questions
documentation:
Iterate over questions and answers for this session.

Yields
------
`tuple`
    A tuple containing the question (`str`) and the answers (`tuple` of
    `str`).

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.wait_for_answer
documentation:
Wait for a correct answer, and then respond.

Scores are also updated in this method.

Returns False if waiting was cancelled; this is usually due to the
session being forcibly stopped.

# Parameters

answers

: A list of valid answers to the current question.

delay

: How long users have to respond (in seconds).

timeout

: How long before the session ends due to no responses (in seconds).

# Returns

bool

: {code}`True` if the session wasn't interrupted.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.check_answer
documentation:
Get a predicate to check for correct answers.

The returned predicate takes a message as its only parameter,
and returns `True` if the message contains any of the
given answers.

# Parameters

answers

: The answers which the predicate must check for.

# Returns

function

: The message predicate.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.end_game
documentation: End the trivia session and display scores.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.send_table
documentation: Send a table of scores to the session's channel.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.stop
documentation: Stop the trivia session, without showing scores.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.force_stop
documentation: Cancel whichever tasks this session is running.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.session.TriviaSession.pay_winners
documentation:
Pay the winner(s) of this trivia session.

Payout only occurs if there are at least 3 human contestants.
If a tie occurs the payout is split evenly among the winners.

Parameters
----------
multiplier : float
    The coefficient of the winning score, used to determine the amount
    paid.

Class to run a session of trivia with the user.

To run the trivia session immediately, use `TriviaSession.start` instead of
instantiating directly.

# Attributes

ctx

: Context object from which this session will be run.
  This object assumes the session was started in `ctx.channel`
  by `ctx.author`.

question_list

: A list of tuples mapping questions (`str`) to answers (`list` of
  `str`).

settings
: Settings for the trivia session, with values for the following:
  : - `max_score` (`int`)
    - `delay` (`float`)
    - `timeout` (`float`)
    - `reveal_answer` (`bool`)
    - `bot_plays` (`bool`)
    - `allow_override` (`bool`)
    - `payout_multiplier` (`float`)
    - `use_spoilers` (`bool`)

scores

: A counter with the players as keys, and their scores as values. The
  players are of type `discord.Member`.

count

: The number of questions which have been asked.

Please note that you can only edit cases you are
the owner of unless you are a mod, admin or server owner.

If no case number is specified, the latest case will be used.
Browse and manage modlog cases. To manage modlog settings, use `[p]modlogset`.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.image.image.Image.imgur_subreddit
documentation:
Get images from a subreddit.

- `<subreddit>`: The subreddit to get images from.
- `[count]`: The number of images to return (maximum 5). Defaults to 1.
- `[sort_type]`: New, or top results. Defaults to top.
- `[window]`: The timeframe, can be the past day, week, month, year or all. Defaults to day.

documentation: Set the total points required to win.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.triviaset_timelimit
documentation: Set the maximum seconds permitted to answer a question.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.triviaset_stopafter

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.triviaset_use_spoilers
documentation:
Set if bot will display the answers in spoilers.

If enabled, the bot will use spoilers to hide answers.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.triviaset_bot_plays
documentation:
Set whether or not the bot gains points.

If enabled, the bot will gain a point if no one guesses correctly.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.triviaset_reveal_answer
documentation:
Set whether or not the answer is revealed.

If enabled, the bot will reveal the answer if no one guesses correctly
in time.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.triviaset_payout_multiplier
documentation:
Set the payout multiplier.

This can be any positive decimal number. If a user wins trivia when at
least 3 members are playing, they will receive credits. Set to 0 to
disable.

The number of credits is determined by multiplying their total score by
this multiplier.

documentation: Delete a trivia file.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.trivia
documentation:
Start trivia session on the specified category.

You may list multiple categories, in which case the trivia will involve
questions from all of them.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.trivia_stop
documentation: Stop an ongoing trivia session.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.trivia_list

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.trivia_info
documentation: Get information about a trivia category.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.trivia_leaderboard
documentation:
Leaderboard for trivia.

Defaults to the top 10 of this server, sorted by total wins. Use
subcommands for a more customised leaderboard.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.trivia_leaderboard_server
documentation:
Leaderboard for this server.

`<sort_by>` can be any of the following fields:
 - `wins`  : total wins
 - `avg`   : average score
 - `total` : total correct answers
 - `games` : total games played

`<top>` is the number of ranks to show on the leaderboard.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.trivia_leaderboard_global
documentation:
Global trivia leaderboard.

`<sort_by>` can be any of the following fields:
 - `wins`  : total wins
 - `avg`   : average score
 - `total` : total correct answers from all sessions
 - `games` : total games played

`<top>` is the number of ranks to show on the leaderboard.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.send_leaderboard
documentation:
Send the leaderboard from the given data.

# Parameters

ctx

: The context to send the leaderboard to.

data

: The data for the leaderboard. This must map `discord.Member` ->
  `dict`.

key

: The field to sort the data by. Can be `wins`, `total_score`,
  `games` or `average_score`.

top

: The number of members to display on the leaderboard.

# Returns

`list` of `discord.Message`

: The sent leaderboard messages.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.on_trivia_end
documentation:
Event for a trivia session ending.

This method removes the session from this cog's sessions, and
cancels any tasks which it was running.

Parameters
----------
session : TriviaSession
    The session which has just ended.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia.update_leaderboard
documentation:
Update the leaderboard with the given scores.

Parameters
----------
session : TriviaSession

Get the trivia list corresponding to the given category.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.trivia.trivia.Trivia._save_trivia_list
documentation:
Checks and saves a trivia list to data folder.

Parameters
----------
file : discord.Attachment
    A discord message attachment.

Returns
-------
None
Play trivia with friends!

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.modset
documentation: Manage server administration settings.

documentation: Show the current server administration settings.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.hierarchy
documentation:
Toggle role hierarchy check for mods and admins.

**WARNING**: Disabling this setting will allow mods to take
actions on users above them in the role hierarchy!

This is enabled by default.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.mentionspam
documentation: Manage the automoderation settings for mentionspam.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.mentionspam_strict
documentation:
Setting to account for duplicate mentions.

If enabled all mentions will count including duplicated mentions.
If disabled only unique mentions will count.

Use this command without any parameter to see current setting.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.mentionspam_warn
documentation:
Sets the autowarn conditions for mention spam.

Users will be warned if they send any messages which contain more than
`<max_mentions>` mentions.

`<max_mentions>` Must be 0 or greater. Set to 0 to disable this feature.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.mentionspam_kick
documentation:
Sets the autokick conditions for mention spam.

Users will be kicked if they send any messages which contain more than
`<max_mentions>` mentions.

`<max_mentions>` Must be 0 or greater. Set to 0 to disable this feature.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.mentionspam_ban
documentation:
Set the autoban conditions for mention spam.

Users will be banned if they send any message which contains more than
`<max_mentions>` mentions.

`<max_mentions>` Must be 0 or greater. Set to 0 to disable this feature.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.deleterepeats
documentation:
Enable auto-deletion of repeated messages.

Must be between 2 and 20.

Set to -1 to disable this feature.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.reinvite
documentation:
Toggle whether an invite will be sent to a user when unbanned.

If this is True, the bot will attempt to create and send a single-use invite
to the newly-unbanned user.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.dm
documentation:
Toggle whether a message should be sent to a user when they are kicked/banned.

If this option is enabled, the bot will attempt to DM the user with the guild name
and reason as to why they were kicked/banned.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.requirereason
documentation:
Toggle whether a reason is required for mod actions.

If this is enabled, the bot will require a reason to be provided for all mod actions.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.defaultdays
documentation:
Set the default number of days worth of messages to be deleted when a user is banned.

The number of days must be between 0 and 7.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.defaultduration
documentation:
Set the default time to be used when a user is tempbanned.

Accepts: seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks
`duration` must be greater than zero.

Examples:
    `[p]modset defaultduration 7d12h10m`
    `[p]modset defaultduration 7 days 12 hours 10 minutes`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.tracknicknames
documentation:
Toggle whether server nickname changes should be tracked.

This setting will be overridden if trackallnames is disabled.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.trackallnames
documentation:
Toggle whether all name changes should be tracked.

Toggling this off also overrides the tracknicknames setting.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.settings.ModSettings.deletenames
documentation:
Delete all stored usernames, global display names, and server nicknames.

Examples:
- `[p]modset deletenames` - Did not confirm. Shows the help message.
- `[p]modset deletenames yes` - Deletes all stored usernames, global display names, and server nicknames.

**Arguments**

- `<confirmation>` This will default to false unless specified.
This is a mixin for the mod cog containing all settings commands.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.get_invite_for_reinvite
documentation: Handles the reinvite logic for getting an invite to send the newly unbanned user

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin._voice_perm_check
documentation:
Check if the bot and user have sufficient permissions for voicebans.

This also verifies that the user's voice state and connected
channel are not `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the permissions are sufficient and the user has
  a valid voice state.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.kick
documentation:
Kick a user.

Examples:
- `[p]kick 428675506947227648 wanted to be kicked.`
    This will kick the user with ID 428675506947227648 from the server.
- `[p]kick @Twentysix wanted to be kicked.`
    This will kick Twentysix from the server.

If a reason is specified, it will be the reason that shows up
in the audit log.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.ban
documentation:
Ban a user from this server and optionally delete days of messages.

`days` is the amount of days of messages to cleanup on ban.

Examples:
- `[p]ban 428675506947227648 7 Continued to spam after told to stop.`
    This will ban the user with ID 428675506947227648 and it will delete 7 days worth of messages.
- `[p]ban @Twentysix 7 Continued to spam after told to stop.`
    This will ban Twentysix and it will delete 7 days worth of messages.

A user ID should be provided if the user is not a member of this server.
If days is not a number, it's treated as the first word of the reason.
Minimum 0 days, maximum 7. If not specified, the defaultdays setting will be used instead.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.massban
documentation:
Mass bans user(s) from the server.

`days` is the amount of days of messages to cleanup on massban.

Example:
   - `[p]massban 345628097929936898 57287406247743488 7 they broke all rules.`
    This will ban all the added userids and delete 7 days worth of their messages.

User IDs need to be provided in order to ban
using this command.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.tempban
documentation:
Temporarily ban a user from this server.

`duration` is the amount of time the user should be banned for.
`days` is the amount of days of messages to cleanup on tempban.

Examples:
- `[p]tempban @Twentysix Because I say so`
    This will ban Twentysix for the default amount of time set by an administrator.
- `[p]tempban @Twentysix 15m You need a timeout`
    This will ban Twentysix for 15 minutes.
- `[p]tempban 428675506947227648 1d2h15m 5 Evil person`
    This will ban the user with ID 428675506947227648 for 1 day 2 hours 15 minutes and will delete the last 5 days of their messages.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.softban
documentation: Kick a user and delete 1 day's worth of their messages.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.voicekick
documentation: Kick a member from a voice channel.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.voiceunban
documentation: Unban a user from speaking and listening in the server's voice channels.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.voiceban
documentation: Ban a user from speaking and listening in the server's voice channels.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mod.kickban.KickBanMixin.unban
documentation:
Unban a user from this server.

Requires specifying the target user's ID. To find this, you may either:
1. Copy it from the mod log case (if one was created), or
2. Enable Developer Mode, go to Bans in this server's settings, right-click the user and select 'Copy ID'.
Kick and ban commands and tasks go here.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.info_schemas.UseDefault
documentation: To be used as sentinel.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.filterset
documentation: Base command to manage filter settings.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.filter_default_name
documentation:
Set the nickname for users with a filtered name.

Note that this has no effect if filtering names is disabled
(to toggle, run `[p]filter names`).

The default name used is *John Doe*.

Example:
- `[p]filterset defaultname Missingno`

**Arguments:**

- `<name>` The new nickname to assign.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.filter_ban
documentation:
Set the filter's autoban conditions.

Users will be banned if they send `<count>` filtered words in
`<timeframe>` seconds.

Set both to zero to disable autoban.

Examples:
- `[p]filterset ban 5 5` - Ban users who say 5 filtered words in 5 seconds.
- `[p]filterset ban 2 20` - Ban users who say 2 filtered words in 20 seconds.

**Arguments:**

- `<count>` The amount of filtered words required to trigger a ban.
- `<timeframe>` The period of time in which too many filtered words will trigger a ban.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter._filter
documentation:
Base command to add or remove words from the server filter.

Use double quotes to add or remove sentences.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter._filter_clear
documentation: Clears this server's filter list.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter._global_list
documentation: Send a list of this server's filtered words.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter._filter_channel
documentation:
Base command to add or remove words from the channel filter.

Use double quotes to add or remove sentences.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter._channel_clear
documentation: Clears this channel's filter list.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter._channel_list
documentation: Send a list of the channel's filtered words.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.filter_channel_add
documentation:
Add words to the filter.

Use double quotes to add sentences.

Examples:
- `[p]filter channel add #channel word1 word2 word3`
- `[p]filter channel add #channel "This is a sentence"`

**Arguments:**

- `<channel>` The text, voice, stage, or forum channel to add filtered words to.
- `[words...]` The words or sentences to filter.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.filter_channel_remove
documentation:
Remove words from the filter.

Use double quotes to remove sentences.

Examples:
- `[p]filter channel remove #channel word1 word2 word3`
- `[p]filter channel remove #channel "This is a sentence"`

**Arguments:**

- `<channel>` The text, voice, stage, or forum channel to add filtered words to.
- `[words...]` The words or sentences to no longer filter.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.filter_add
documentation:
Add words to the filter.

Use double quotes to add sentences.

Examples:
- `[p]filter add word1 word2 word3`
- `[p]filter add "This is a sentence"`

**Arguments:**

- `[words...]` The words or sentences to filter.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.filter_remove
documentation:
Remove words from the filter.

Use double quotes to remove sentences.

Examples:
- `[p]filter remove word1 word2 word3`
- `[p]filter remove "This is a sentence"`

**Arguments:**

- `[words...]` The words or sentences to no longer filter.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.filter_names
documentation:
Toggle name and nickname filtering.

This is disabled by default.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.filter.filter.Filter.invalidate_cache
documentation: Invalidate a cached pattern

This cog is designed for "filtering" unwanted words and phrases from a server.

It provides tools to manage a list of words or sentences, and to customize automatic actions to be taken against users who use those words in channels or in their name/nickname.

This can be used to prevent inappropriate language, off-topic discussions, invite links, and more.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.register_warningtype
documentation: We're not utilising modlog yet - no need to register a casetype

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.warningset
documentation: Manage settings for Warnings.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.allowcustomreasons
documentation: Enable or disable custom reasons for a warning.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.senddm
documentation: Set whether warnings should be sent to users in DMs.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.showmoderator
documentation: Decide whether the name of the moderator warning a user should be included in the DM to that user.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.warnchannel
documentation:
Set the channel where warnings should be sent to.

Leave empty to use the channel `[p]warn` command was called in.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.usewarnchannel
documentation: Set if warnings should be sent to a channel set with `[p]warningset warnchannel`.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.warnaction
documentation:
Manage automated actions for Warnings.

Actions are essentially command macros. Any command can be run
when the action is initially triggered, and/or when the action
is lifted.
Actions must be given a name and a points threshold. When a
user is warned enough so that their points go over this
threshold, the action will be executed.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.action_add
documentation:
Create an automated action.

Duplicate action names are not allowed.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.action_del
documentation: Delete the action with the specified name.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.warnreason
documentation:
Manage warning reasons.

Reasons must be given a name, description and points value. The
name of the reason must be given when a user is warned.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.reason_create
documentation: Create a warning reason.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.reason_del
documentation: Delete a warning reason.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.reasonlist
documentation: List all configured reasons for Warnings.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.actionlist
documentation: List all configured automated actions for Warnings.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.warn
documentation:
Warn the user for the specified reason.

`<points>` number of points the warning should be for. If no number is supplied
1 point will be given. Pre-set warnings disregard this.
`<reason>` is reason for the warning. This can be a registered reason,
or a custom reason if `[p]warningset allowcustomreasons` is set.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.warnings
documentation: List the warnings for the specified user.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.mywarnings
documentation: List warnings for yourself.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.warnings.warnings.Warnings.unwarn
documentation: Remove a warning from a user.
Warn misbehaving users and take automated actions.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.installable.Installable

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.installable.Installable.__init__
documentation:
Base installable initializer.

Parameters
----------
location : pathlib.Path
    Location (file or folder) to the installable.
repo : Repo, optional
    Repo object of the Installable, if repo is missing this will be `None`
commit : str
    Installable's commit. This is not the same as ``repo.commit``

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.installable.Installable.name
documentation: `str` : The name of this package.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.installable.Installable.copy_to
documentation:
Copies this cog/shared_lib to the given directory. This
will overwrite any files in the target directory.

```
param pathlib.Path target_dir

The installation directory to install to.

return

Status of installation

rtype

bool
```

Base class for anything the Downloader cog can install.

> - Modules
> - Repo Libraries
> - Other stuff?

The attributes of this class will mostly come from the installation's
info.json.

# Attributes

repo_name

: Name of the repository which this package belongs to.

repo

: Repo object of the Installable, if repo is missing this will be `None`

commit

: Installable's commit. This is not the same as `repo.commit`

author

: Name(s) of the author(s).

end_user_data_statement

: End user data statement of the module.

min_bot_version

: The minimum bot version required for this Installable.

max_bot_version

: The maximum bot version required for this Installable.
  Ignored if `min_bot_version` is newer than `max_bot_version`.

min_python_version

: The minimum python version required for this cog.

hidden

: Whether or not this cog will be hidden from the user when they use
  `Downloader`'s commands.

required_cogs

: In the form {code}`{cog_name : repo_url}`, these are cogs which are
  required for this installation.

requirements

: Required libraries for this installation.

tags

: List of tags to assist in searching.

type

: The type of this installation, as specified by
  {class}`InstallationType`.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.installable.InstalledModule
documentation:
Base class for installed modules,
this is basically instance of installed `Installable`
used by Downloader.

Attributes

pinned : `bool`
    Whether or not this cog is pinned, always `False` if module is not a cog.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.repo_manager.Repo.is_ancestor

Check if the first is an ancestor of the second.

descendant_rev

: Descendant revision

  ancestor of {code}`descendant_rev` or `False` otherwise

new_rev : `str`, optional

Gets module's `Installable` from last commit in which it still occurs.

Parameters

descendant_rev : `str`, optional

Returns

`Installable`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.repo_manager.Repo._is_module_modified
documentation:
Checks if given module was different in {code}`other_hash`.

other_hash

# Returns

bool

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.repo_manager.Repo.get_modified_modules
documentation:
Gets modified modules between the two revisions.
For every module that doesn't exist in {code}`new_rev`,
it will try to find last commit, where it still existed

# Parameters

old_rev

: Pre-update revision, ancestor of {code}`new_rev`

new_rev

: Post-update revision, defaults to repo's branch if not given

# Returns

`tuple` of `Installable`

: List of changed modules between the two revisions.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.repo_manager.Repo._get_commit_notes
documentation:
Gets the commit notes from git log.

\:param relative_file_path: Path relative to the repo folder of the file

Git commit note log

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.repo_manager.Repo._update_available_modules

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.repo_manager.Repo._run
documentation:
# Parameters

valid_exit_codes

: Specifies valid exit codes, used to determine
  if stderr should be sent as debug or error level in logging.
  When not provided, defaults to {code}`(0,)`

debug_only

: Specifies if stderr can be sent only as debug level in logging.
  When not provided, defaults to `False`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.repo_manager.Repo.checkout
documentation:
Checks out repository to provided revision.

The return value of this method can also be used as an asynchronous
context manager, i.e. with {code}`async with` syntax. This will
checkout repository to {code}`exit_to_rev` on exit of the context manager.

# Parameters

rev

: Revision to checkout to, when not provided, method won't do anything

exit_to_rev

: Revision to checkout to after exiting context manager,
  when not provided, defaults to current commit
  This will be ignored, when used with {code}`await` or when {code}`rev` is `None`.

force_checkout

: When `True` checkout will be done even
  if {code}`self.commit` is the same as target hash
  (applies to exiting context manager as well)
  If provided revision isn't full sha1 hash,
  checkout will be done no matter to this parameter.
  Defaults to `False`.

# Raises

.UnknownRevision

: When git cannot checkout to provided revision.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.red_delete_data_for_user
documentation: Nothing to delete

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.check_100_plus
documentation:
Called when trying to delete more than 100 messages at once.

Prompts the user to choose whether they want to continue or not.

Tries its best to cleanup after itself if the response is positive.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.get_messages_for_deletion
documentation:
Gets a list of messages meeting the requirements to be deleted.
Generally, the requirements are:
- We don't have the number of messages to be deleted already
- The message passes a provided check (if no check is provided,
  this is automatically true)
- The message is less than 14 days old
- The message is not pinned

Warning: Due to the way the API hands messages back in chunks,
passing after and a number together is not advisable.
If you need to accomplish this, you should filter messages on
the entire applicable range, rather than use this utility.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.send_optional_notification
documentation: Sends a notification to the channel that a certain number of messages have been deleted.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.cleanup
documentation: Base command for deleting messages.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.text
documentation:
Delete the last X messages matching the specified text in the current channel.

Example:
- `[p]cleanup text "test" 5`

Remember to use double quotes.

**Arguments:**

- `<number>` The max number of messages to cleanup. Must be a positive integer.
- `<delete_pinned>` Whether to delete pinned messages or not. Defaults to False

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.user
documentation:
Delete the last X messages from a specified user in the current channel.

Examples:
- `[p]cleanup user @Twentysix 2`
- `[p]cleanup user Red 6`

**Arguments:**

- `<user>` The user whose messages are to be cleaned up.
- `<number>` The max number of messages to cleanup. Must be a positive integer.
- `<delete_pinned>` Whether to delete pinned messages or not. Defaults to False

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.after
documentation:
Delete all messages after a specified message.

To get a message id, enable developer mode in Discord's
settings, 'appearance' tab. Then right click a message
and copy its id.
Replying to a message will cleanup all messages after it.

**Arguments:**

- `<message_id>` The id of the message to cleanup after. This message won't be deleted.
- `<delete_pinned>` Whether to delete pinned messages or not. Defaults to False

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.before
documentation:
Deletes X messages before the specified message.

To get a message id, enable developer mode in Discord's
settings, 'appearance' tab. Then right click a message
and copy its id.
Replying to a message will cleanup all messages before it.

**Arguments:**

- `<message_id>` The id of the message to cleanup before. This message won't be deleted.
- `<number>` The max number of messages to cleanup. Must be a positive integer.
- `<delete_pinned>` Whether to delete pinned messages or not. Defaults to False

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.between
documentation:
Delete the messages between Message One and Message Two, providing the messages IDs.

The first message ID should be the older message and the second one the newer.

Example:
- `[p]cleanup between 123456789123456789 987654321987654321`

**Arguments:**

- `<one>` The id of the message to cleanup after. This message won't be deleted.
- `<two>` The id of the message to cleanup before. This message won't be deleted.
- `<delete_pinned>` Whether to delete pinned messages or not. Defaults to False

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.messages
documentation:
Delete the last X messages in the current channel.

Example:
- `[p]cleanup messages 26`

**Arguments:**

- `<number>` The max number of messages to cleanup. Must be a positive integer.
- `<delete_pinned>` Whether to delete pinned messages or not. Defaults to False

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.cleanup_bot
documentation:
Clean up command messages and messages from the bot in the current channel.

Can only cleanup custom commands and alias commands if those cogs are loaded.

**Arguments:**

- `<number>` The max number of messages to cleanup. Must be a positive integer.
- `<delete_pinned>` Whether to delete pinned messages or not. Defaults to False

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.cleanup_self
documentation:
Clean up messages owned by the bot in the current channel.

By default, all messages are cleaned. If a second argument is specified,
it is used for pattern matching - only messages containing the given text will be deleted.

Examples:
- `[p]cleanup self 6`
- `[p]cleanup self 10 Pong`
- `[p]cleanup self 7 "" True`

**Arguments:**

- `<number>` The max number of messages to cleanup. Must be a positive integer.
- `<match_pattern>` The text that messages must contain to be deleted. Use "" to skip this.
- `<delete_pinned>` Whether to delete pinned messages or not. Defaults to False

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.cleanup_duplicates
documentation:
Deletes duplicate messages in the channel from the last X messages and keeps only one copy.

Defaults to 50.

**Arguments:**

- `<number>` The number of messages to check for duplicates. Must be a positive integer.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.cleanupset
documentation: Manage the settings for the cleanup command.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.cleanup.cleanup.Cleanup.cleanupset_notify
documentation:
Toggle clean up notification settings.

When enabled, a message will be sent per cleanup, showing how many messages were deleted.
This message will be deleted after 5 seconds.

This cog contains commands used for "cleaning up" (deleting) messages.

This is designed as a moderator tool and offers many convenient use cases.
All cleanup commands only apply to the channel the command is executed in.

Messages older than two weeks cannot be mass deleted.
This is a limitation of the API.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CommandObj.create

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CommandObj.edit

classes.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.customcom
documentation: Base command for Custom Commands management.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_raw
documentation:
Get the raw response of a custom command, to get the proper markdown.

This is helpful for copy and pasting.

**Arguments:**

- `<command>` The custom command to get the raw response of.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_search
documentation:
Searches through custom commands, according to the query.

Uses fuzzy searching to find close matches.

**Arguments:**

- `<query>` The query to search for. Can be multiple words.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_create
documentation:
Create custom commands.

If a type is not specified, a simple CC will be created.
CCs can be enhanced with arguments, see the guide
[here](https://docs.discord.red/en/stable/cog_customcom.html).

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_create_random
documentation:
Create a CC where it will randomly choose a response!

Note: This command is interactive.

**Arguments:**

- `<command>` The command executed to return the text. Cast to lowercase.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_create_simple
documentation:
Add a simple custom command.

Example:
- `[p]customcom create simple yourcommand Text you want`

**Arguments:**

- `<command>` The command executed to return the text. Cast to lowercase.
- `<text>` The text to return when executing the command. See guide for enhanced usage.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_cooldown
documentation:
Set, edit, or view the cooldown for a custom command.

You may set cooldowns per member, channel, or guild. Multiple
cooldowns may be set. All cooldowns must be cooled to call the
custom command.

Examples:
- `[p]customcom cooldown pingrole`
- `[p]customcom cooldown yourcommand 30`
- `[p]cc cooldown mycommand 30 guild`

**Arguments:**

- `<command>` The custom command to check or set the cooldown.
- `[cooldown]` The number of seconds to wait before allowing the command to be invoked again. If omitted, will instead return the current cooldown settings.
- `[per]` The group to apply the cooldown on. Defaults to per member. Valid choices are server / guild, user / member, and channel.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_delete
documentation:
Delete a custom command.

Example:
- `[p]customcom delete yourcommand`

**Arguments:**

- `<command>` The custom command to delete.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_edit
documentation:
Edit a custom command.

Example:
- `[p]customcom edit yourcommand Text you want`

**Arguments:**

- `<command>` The custom command to edit.
- `<text>` The new text to return when executing the command.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_list
documentation:
List all available custom commands.

The list displays a preview of each command's response, with
markdown escaped and newlines replaced with spaces.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_show
documentation:
Shows a custom command's responses and its settings.

**Arguments:**

- `<command_name>` The custom command to show.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.cc_callback
documentation:
Custom command.

Created via the CustomCom cog. See `[p]customcom` for more details.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.transform_parameter
documentation:
For security reasons only specific objects are allowed
Internals are ignored

methods.name: redbot.cogs.customcom.customcom.CustomCommands.get_command_names
documentation:
Get all custom command names in a guild.

Returns
--------
Set[str]
    A set of all custom command names.

This cog contains commands for creating and managing custom commands that display text.

These are useful for storing information members might need, like FAQ answers or invite links.
Custom commands can be used by anyone by default, so be careful with pings.
Commands can only be lowercase, and will not respond to any uppercase letters.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader.pipinstall
documentation:
Install a group of dependencies using pip.

Examples:
- `[p]pipinstall bs4`
- `[p]pipinstall py-cpuinfo psutil`

Improper usage of this command can break your bot, be careful.

**Arguments**

- `<deps...>` The package or packages you wish to install.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader.repo
documentation: Base command for repository management.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._repo_add
documentation:
Add a new repo.

Examples:
- `[p]repo add 26-Cogs https://github.com/Twentysix26/x26-Cogs`
- `[p]repo add Laggrons-Dumb-Cogs https://github.com/retke/Laggrons-Dumb-Cogs v3`

Repo names can only contain characters A-z, numbers, underscores, hyphens, and dots (but they cannot start or end with a dot).

The branch will be the default branch if not specified.

**Arguments**

- `<name>` The name given to the repo.
- `<repo_url>` URL to the cog branch. Usually GitHub or GitLab.
- `[branch]` Optional branch to install cogs from.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._repo_del
documentation:
Remove repos and their files.

Examples:
- `[p]repo delete 26-Cogs`
- `[p]repo delete 26-Cogs Laggrons-Dumb-Cogs`

**Arguments**

- `<repos...>` The repo or repos to remove.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._repo_list
documentation: List all installed repos.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._repo_info
documentation:
Show information about a repo.

Example:
- `[p]repo info 26-Cogs`

**Arguments**

- `<repo>` The name of the repo to show info about.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._repo_update
documentation:
Update all repos, or ones of your choosing.

This will *not* update the cogs installed from those repos.

Examples:
- `[p]repo update`
- `[p]repo update 26-Cogs`
- `[p]repo update 26-Cogs Laggrons-Dumb-Cogs`

**Arguments**

- `[repos...]` The name or names of repos to update. If omitted, all repos are updated.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader.cog
documentation: Base command for cog installation management commands.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_reinstallreqs
documentation:
This command should not be used unless Red specifically asks for it.

This command will reinstall cog requirements and shared libraries for all installed cogs.

Red might ask the owner to use this when it clears contents of the lib folder
because of change in minor version of Python.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_install
documentation:
Install a cog from the given repo.

Examples:
- `[p]cog install 26-Cogs defender`
- `[p]cog install Laggrons-Dumb-Cogs say roleinvite`

**Arguments**

- `<repo>` The name of the repo to install cogs from.
- `<cogs...>` The cog or cogs to install.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_installversion
documentation:
Install a cog from the specified revision of given repo.

Revisions are "commit ids" that point to the point in the code when a specific change was made.
The latest revision can be found in the URL bar for any GitHub repo by [pressing "y" on that repo](https://docs.github.com/en/free-pro-team@latest/github/managing-files-in-a-repository/getting-permanent-links-to-files#press-y-to-permalink-to-a-file-in-a-specific-commit).

Older revisions can be found in the URL bar by [viewing the commit history of any repo](https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/133251234164375552/775760247787749406/unknown.png)

Example:
- `[p]cog installversion Broken-Repo e798cc268e199612b1316a3d1f193da0770c7016 cog_name`

**Arguments**

- `<repo>` The name of the repo to install cogs from.
- `<revision>` The revision to install from.
- `<cogs...>` The cog or cogs to install.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_uninstall
documentation:
Uninstall cogs.

You may only uninstall cogs which were previously installed
by Downloader.

Examples:
- `[p]cog uninstall defender`
- `[p]cog uninstall say roleinvite`

**Arguments**

- `<cogs...>` The cog or cogs to uninstall.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_pin
documentation:
Pin cogs - this will lock cogs on their current version.

Examples:
- `[p]cog pin defender`
- `[p]cog pin outdated_cog1 outdated_cog2`

**Arguments**

- `<cogs...>` The cog or cogs to pin. Must already be installed.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_unpin
documentation:
Unpin cogs - this will remove the update lock from those cogs.

Examples:
- `[p]cog unpin defender`
- `[p]cog unpin updated_cog1 updated_cog2`

**Arguments**

- `<cogs...>` The cog or cogs to unpin. Must already be installed and pinned.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_listpinned
documentation: List currently pinned cogs.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_checkforupdates
documentation:
Check for available cog updates (including pinned cogs).

This command doesn't update cogs, it only checks for updates.
Use `[p]cog update` to update cogs.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_update
documentation:
Update all cogs, or ones of your choosing.

Examples:
- `[p]cog update`
- `[p]cog update True`
- `[p]cog update defender`
- `[p]cog update True defender`

**Arguments**

- `[reload]` Whether to reload cogs immediately after update or not.
- `[cogs...]` The cog or cogs to update. If omitted, all cogs are updated.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_updateallfromrepos
documentation:
Update all cogs from repos of your choosing.

Examples:
- `[p]cog updateallfromrepos 26-Cogs`
- `[p]cog updateallfromrepos True 26-Cogs`
- `[p]cog updateallfromrepos Laggrons-Dumb-Cogs 26-Cogs`

**Arguments**

- `[reload]` Whether to reload cogs immediately after update or not.
- `<repos...>` The repo or repos to update all cogs from.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_updatetoversion
documentation:
Update all cogs, or ones of your choosing to chosen revision of one repo.

Note that update doesn't mean downgrade and therefore `revision`
has to be newer than the version that cog currently has installed. If you want to
downgrade the cog, uninstall and install it again.

See `[p]cog installversion` for an explanation of `revision`.

Examples:
- `[p]cog updatetoversion Broken-Repo e798cc268e199612b1316a3d1f193da0770c7016 cog_name`
- `[p]cog updatetoversion True Broken-Repo 6107c0770ad391f1d3a6131b216991e862cc897e cog_name`

**Arguments**

- `[reload]` Whether to reload cogs immediately after update or not.
- `<repo>` The repo or repos to update all cogs from.
- `<revision>` The revision to update to.
- `[cogs...]` The cog or cogs to update.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_list
documentation:
List all available cogs from a single repo.

Example:
- `[p]cog list 26-Cogs`

**Arguments**

- `<repo>` The repo to list cogs from.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._cog_info
documentation:
List information about a single cog.

Example:
- `[p]cog info 26-Cogs defender`

**Arguments**

- `<repo>` The repo to get cog info from.
- `<cog>` The cog to get info on.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader.is_installed
documentation:
Check to see if a cog has been installed through Downloader.

# Parameters

cog_name

: The name of the cog to check for.

# Returns

`tuple` of (`bool`, `InstalledModule`)

: {code}`(True, InstalledModule)` if the cog is installed, else
  {code}`(False, None)`.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader._filter_incorrect_cogs_by_names
documentation:
Filter out incorrect cogs from list.

Parameters
----------
repo : `Repo`
    Repo which should be searched for `cog_names`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader.cog_name_from_instance
documentation:
Determines the cog name that Downloader knows from the cog instance.

Probably.

Parameters
----------
instance : object
    The cog instance.

Returns
-------
str
    The name of the cog according to Downloader..

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader.findcog
documentation:
Find which cog a command comes from.

This will only work with loaded cogs.

Example:
- `[p]findcog ping`

**Arguments**

- `<command_name>` The command to search for.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.downloader.downloader.Downloader.format_failed_repos
documentation:
Format collection of `Repo.name`'s into failed message.

# Parameters

failed

: Collection of `Repo.name`

# Returns

str

: formatted message

Install community cogs made by Cog Creators.

Community cogs, also called third party cogs, are not included
in the default Red install.

Community cogs come in repositories. Repos are a group of cogs
you can install. You always need to add the creator's repository
using the `[p]repo` command before you can install one or more
cogs from the creator.

Create aliases for commands.

Aliases are alternative names/shortcuts for commands. They
can act as both a lambda (storing arguments for repeated use)
or as simply a shortcut to saying "x y z".

When run, aliases will accept any additional arguments
and append them to the stored alias.

classes.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy._bank
documentation: Base command to manage the bank.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.balance
documentation:
Show the user's account balance.

Example:
- `[p]bank balance`
- `[p]bank balance @Twentysix`

**Arguments**

- `<user>` The user to check the balance of. If omitted, defaults to your own balance.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.transfer
documentation:
Transfer currency to other users.

This will come out of your balance, so make sure you have enough.

Example:
- `[p]bank transfer @Twentysix 500`

**Arguments**

- `<to>` The user to give currency to.
- `<amount>` The amount of currency to give.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy._set
documentation:
Set the balance of a user's bank account.

Putting + or - signs before the amount will add/remove currency on the user's bank account instead.

Examples:
- `[p]bank set @Twentysix 26` - Sets balance to 26
- `[p]bank set @Twentysix +2` - Increases balance by 2
- `[p]bank set @Twentysix -6` - Decreases balance by 6

**Arguments**

- `<to>` The user to set the currency of.
- `<creds>` The amount of currency to set their balance to.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.payday
documentation:
Get some free currency.

The amount awarded and frequency can be configured.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.leaderboard
documentation:
Print the leaderboard.

Defaults to top 10.

Examples:
- `[p]leaderboard`
- `[p]leaderboard 50` - Shows the top 50 instead of top 10.
- `[p]leaderboard 100 yes` - Shows the top 100 from all servers.

**Arguments**

- `<top>` How many positions on the leaderboard to show. Defaults to 10 if omitted.
- `<show_global>` Whether to include results from all servers. This will default to false unless specified.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.payouts
documentation: Show the payouts for the slot machine.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.slot
documentation:
Use the slot machine.

Example:
- `[p]slot 50`

**Arguments**

- `<bid>` The amount to bet on the slot machine. Winning payouts are higher when you bet more.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.economyset
documentation: Base command to manage Economy settings.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.economyset_showsettings
documentation: Shows the current economy settings

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.slotmin
documentation:
Set the minimum slot machine bid.

Example:
- `[p]economyset slotmin 10`

**Arguments**

- `<bid>` The new minimum bid for using the slot machine. Default is 5.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.slotmax
documentation:
Set the maximum slot machine bid.

Example:
- `[p]economyset slotmax 50`

**Arguments**

- `<bid>` The new maximum bid for using the slot machine. Default is 100.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.slottime
documentation:
Set the cooldown for the slot machine.

Examples:
- `[p]economyset slottime 10`
- `[p]economyset slottime 10m`

**Arguments**

- `<duration>` The new duration to wait in between uses of the slot machine. Default is 5 seconds.
Accepts: seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks (if no unit is specified, the duration is assumed to be given in seconds)

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.paydaytime
documentation:
Set the cooldown for the payday command.

Examples:
- `[p]economyset paydaytime 86400`
- `[p]economyset paydaytime 1d`

**Arguments**

- `<duration>` The new duration to wait in between uses of payday. Default is 5 minutes.
Accepts: seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks (if no unit is specified, the duration is assumed to be given in seconds)

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.paydayamount
documentation:
Set the amount earned each payday.

Example:
- `[p]economyset paydayamount 400`

**Arguments**

- `<creds>` The new amount to give when using the payday command. Default is 120.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.economy.economy.Economy.rolepaydayamount
documentation:
Set the amount earned each payday for a role.

Set to `0` to remove the payday amount you set for that role.

Only available when not using a global bank.

Example:
- `[p]economyset rolepaydayamount @Members 400`

**Arguments**

- `<role>` The role to assign a custom payday amount to.
- `<creds>` The new amount to give when using the payday command.
Get rich and have fun with imaginary currency!

methods.name: redbot.cogs.streams.streams.Streams.with_mention
documentation:
Set stream alert message when mentions are enabled.

Use `{mention}` in the message to insert the selected mentions.
Use `{stream}` in the message to insert the channel or username.
Use `{stream.display_name}` in the message to insert the channel's display name (on Twitch, this may be different from `{stream}`).

For example: `[p]streamset message mention {mention}, {stream.display_name} is live!`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.streams.streams.Streams.without_mention
documentation:
Set stream alert message when mentions are disabled.

Use `{stream}` in the message to insert the channel or username.
Use `{stream.display_name}` in the message to insert the channel's display name (on Twitch, this may be different from `{stream}`).

For example: `[p]streamset message nomention {stream.display_name} is live!`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.apis.interface.AudioAPIInterface.fetch_track
documentation:
A replacement for {code}`lavalink.Player.load_tracks`. This will try to get a valid
cached entry first if not found or if in valid it will then call the lavalink API.

# Parameters

ctx: commands.Context

: The context this method is being called under.

player

: The player who's requesting the query.

query: audio_dataclasses.Query

: The Query object for the query in question.

forced:bool

: Whether or not to skip cache and call API first.

lazy:bool

: If set to True, it will not call the api if a track is not found.

should_query_global:bool

: If the method should query the global database.

# Returns

Tuple[lavalink.LoadResult, bool]

: Tuple with the Load result and whether or not the API was called.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.localtracks.LocalTrackCommands.command_local_folder
documentation:
Play all songs in a localtracks folder.

**Usage**:
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]local folder`
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Open a menu to pick a folder to queue.

​ ​ `[p]local folder folder_name`
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Queues all of the tracks inside the folder_name folder.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.localtracks.LocalTrackCommands.command_local_play
documentation:
Play a local track.

To play a local track, either use the menu to choose a track or enter in the track path directly with the play command.
To play an entire folder, use `[p]help local folder` for instructions.

**Usage**:
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]local play`
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Open a menu to pick a track.

​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]play localtracks\album_folder\song_name.mp3`
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]play album_folder\song_name.mp3`
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Use a direct link relative to the localtracks folder.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.equalizer.EqualizerCommands.command_equalizer
documentation:
Equalizer management.

Band positions are 1-15 and values have a range of -0.25 to 1.0.
Band names are 25, 40, 63, 100, 160, 250, 400, 630, 1k, 1.6k, 2.5k, 4k,
6.3k, 10k, and 16k Hz.
Setting a band value to -0.25 nullifies it while +0.25 is double.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.equalizer.EqualizerCommands.command_equalizer_delete
documentation: Delete a saved eq preset.

documentation: List saved eq presets.

documentation: Load a saved eq preset.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.equalizer.EqualizerCommands.command_equalizer_reset
documentation: Reset the eq to 0 across all bands.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.equalizer.EqualizerCommands.command_equalizer_save
documentation: Save the current eq settings to a preset.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.equalizer.EqualizerCommands.command_equalizer_set
documentation:
Set an eq band with a band number or name and value.

Band positions are 1-15 and values have a range of -0.25 to 1.0.
Band names are 25, 40, 63, 100, 160, 250, 400, 630, 1k, 1.6k, 2.5k, 4k,
6.3k, 10k, and 16k Hz.
Setting a band value to -0.25 nullifies it while +0.25 is double.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset
documentation:
`Dangerous commands` Manage Lavalink node configuration settings.

This command block holds all commands to configure an unmanaged (user maintained) or managed (bot maintained) Lavalink node.

You should not mess with any command in here unless you have a valid reason to,
i.e. been told by someone in the Red-Discord Bot support server to do so.
All the commands in here have the potential to break the Audio cog.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_java
documentation:
Change your Java executable path.

This command shouldn't need to be used most of the time, and is only useful if the host machine has conflicting Java versions.

If changing this make sure that the Java executable you set is supported by Audio.
The current supported versions are Java 17 and 11.

Enter nothing or "java" to reset it back to default.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_heapsize
documentation:
Set the managed Lavalink node maximum heap-size.

By default, this value is 50% of available RAM in the host machine represented by [1-1024][M|G] (256M, 256G for example)

This value only represents the maximum amount of RAM allowed to be used at any given point, and does not mean that the managed Lavalink node will always use this amount of RAM.

To reset this value to the default, run the command without any input.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_unmanaged
documentation:
Toggle using external (unmanaged) Lavalink nodes - requires an existing Lavalink node for Audio to work, if enabled.

This command disables the managed Lavalink server. If you do not have another Lavalink node set up, you will be unable to use Audio while this is enabled.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_host
documentation:
Set the Lavalink node host.

This command sets the connection host which Audio will use to connect to an unmanaged Lavalink node.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_password
documentation:
Set the Lavalink node password.

This command sets the connection password which Audio will use to connect to an unmanaged Lavalink node.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_wsport
documentation:
Set the Lavalink node port.

This command sets the connection port which Audio will use to connect to an unmanaged Lavalink node.
Set port to -1 to disable the port and connect to the specified host via ports 80/443

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_secured
documentation:
Set the Lavalink node connection to secured.

This toggle sets the connection type to secured or unsecured when connecting to an unmanaged Lavalink node.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_info
documentation: Display Lavalink connection settings.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_yaml
documentation: Uploads a copy of the application.yml file used by the managed Lavalink node.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config
documentation:
Configure the managed Lavalink node runtime options.

All settings under this group will likely cause Audio to malfunction if changed from their defaults, only change settings here if you have been advised to by support.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_server
documentation: Configure the managed node authorization and connection settings.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_host
documentation:
`Dangerous command` Set the managed Lavalink node's binding IP address.

This value by default is `localhost` which will restrict the server to only localhost apps by default, changing this will likely break the managed Lavalink node if you don't know what you are doing.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_token
documentation:
Set the managed Lavalink node's connection password.

This is the password required for Audio to connect to the managed Lavalink node.
The value by default is `youshallnotpass`.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_port
documentation:
`Dangerous command` Set the managed Lavalink node's connection port.

This port is the port the managed Lavalink node binds to, you should only change this if there is a conflict with the default port because you already have an application using port 2333 on this device.

The value by default is `2333`.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_source
documentation:
`Dangerous command` Toggle audio sources on/off.

By default, all sources are enabled, you should only use commands here to disable a specific source if you have been advised to, disabling sources without background knowledge can cause Audio to break.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_source_http
documentation:
Toggle HTTP direct URL usage on or off.

This source is used to allow playback from direct HTTP streams (this does not affect direct URL playback for the other sources).

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_source_bandcamp
documentation:
Toggle Bandcamp source on or off.

This toggle controls the playback of all Bandcamp related content.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_source_local
documentation:
Toggle local file usage on or off.

This toggle controls the playback of all local track content, usually found inside the `localtracks` folder.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_source_soundcloud
documentation:
Toggle Soundcloud source on or off.

This toggle controls the playback of all SoundCloud related content.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_source_youtube
documentation:
`Dangerous command` Toggle YouTube source on or off (this includes Spotify).

This toggle controls the playback of all YouTube and Spotify related content.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_source_twitch
documentation:
Toggle Twitch source on or off.

This toggle controls the playback of all Twitch related content.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_source_vimeo
documentation:
Toggle Vimeo source on or off.

This toggle controls the playback of all Vimeo related content.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_server_framebuffer
documentation:
`Dangerous command` Set the managed Lavalink node framebuffer size.

Only change this if you have been directly advised to, changing it can cause significant playback issues.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_config_server_buffer
documentation:
`Dangerous command`  Set the managed Lavalink node JDA-NAS buffer size.

Only change this if you have been directly advised to, changing it can cause significant playback issues.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.llset.LavalinkSetupCommands.command_llset_reset
documentation: Reset all `llset` changes back to their default values.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.reports.reports.Reports.response
documentation:
Open a message tunnel.

This tunnel will forward things you say in this channel or thread
to the ticket opener's direct messages.

Tunnels do not persist across bot restarts.

Create user reports that server staff can respond to.

Users can open reports using `[p]report`. These are then sent
to a channel in the server for staff, and the report creator
gets a DM. Both can be used to communicate.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.playlists.PlaylistCommands.command_playlist
documentation:
Playlist configuration options.

Scope info:
​ ​ ​ ​ **Global**:
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Visible to all users of this bot.
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Only editable by bot owner.
​ ​ ​ ​ **Guild**:
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Visible to all users in this guild.
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Editable by bot owner, guild owner, guild admins, guild mods, DJ role and playlist creator.
​ ​ ​ ​ **User**:
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Visible to all bot users, if --author is passed.
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ Editable by bot owner and the playlist creator.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.playlists.PlaylistCommands.command_playlist_append
documentation:
Add a track URL, playlist link, or quick search to a playlist.

The track(s) will be appended to the end of the playlist.

**Usage**:
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]playlist append playlist_name_OR_id track_name_OR_url [args]`

**Args**:
​ ​ ​ ​ The following are all optional:
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --scope <scope>
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --author [user]
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --guild [guild] **Only the bot owner can use this**

**Scope** is one of the following:
​ ​ ​ ​ Global
​ ​ ​ ​ Guild
​ ​ ​ ​ User

**Author** can be one of the following:
​ ​ ​ ​ User ID
​ ​ ​ ​ User Mention
​ ​ ​ ​ User Name#123

**Guild** can be one of the following:
​ ​ ​ ​ Guild ID
​ ​ ​ ​ Exact guild name

Example use:
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]playlist append MyGuildPlaylist Hello by Adele`
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]playlist append MyGlobalPlaylist Hello by Adele --scope Global`
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]playlist append MyGlobalPlaylist Hello by Adele --scope Global --Author Draper#6666`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.playlists.PlaylistCommands.command_playlist_copy
documentation:
Copy a playlist from one scope to another.

**Usage**:
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]playlist copy playlist_name_OR_id [args]`

**Args**:
​ ​ ​ ​ The following are all optional:
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --from-scope <scope>
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --from-author [user]
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --from-guild [guild] **Only the bot owner can use this**

​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --to-scope <scope>
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --to-author [user]
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --to-guild [guild] **Only the bot owner can use this**

**Scope** is one of the following:
​ ​ ​ ​ Global
​ ​ ​ ​ Guild
​ ​ ​ ​ User

**Author** can be one of the following:
​ ​ ​ ​ User ID
​ ​ ​ ​ User Mention
​ ​ ​ ​ User Name#123

**Guild** can be one of the following:
​ ​ ​ ​ Guild ID
​ ​ ​ ​ Exact guild name

Example use:
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]playlist copy MyGuildPlaylist --from-scope Guild --to-scope Global`
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]playlist copy MyGlobalPlaylist --from-scope Global --to-author Draper#6666 --to-scope User`
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]playlist copy MyPersonalPlaylist --from-scope user --to-author Draper#6666 --to-scope Guild --to-guild Red - Discord Bot`

​ ​ ​ ​ User Mention
​ ​ ​ ​ User Name#123

**Guild** can be one of the following:

classes.name: redbot.cogs.mutes.voicemutes.VoiceMutes

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mutes.voicemutes.VoiceMutes._voice_perm_check
documentation:
Check if the bot and user have sufficient permissions for voicebans.

This also verifies that the user's voice state and connected
channel are not `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the permissions are sufficient and the user has
  a valid voice state.

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mutes.voicemutes.VoiceMutes.voice_mute
documentation:
Mute a user in their current voice channel.

`<users...>` is a space separated list of usernames, ID's, or mentions.
`[time_and_reason]` is the time to mute for and reason. Time is
any valid time length such as `30 minutes` or `2 days`. If nothing
is provided the mute will use the set default time or indefinite if not set.

Examples:
`[p]voicemute @member1 @member2 spam 5 hours`
`[p]voicemute @member1 3 days`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.mutes.voicemutes.VoiceMutes.unmute_voice
documentation:
Unmute a user in their current voice channel.

`<users...>` is a space separated list of usernames, ID's, or mentions.
`[reason]` is the reason for the unmute.
This handles all voice channel related muting

methods.name: redbot.cogs.audio.core.commands.audioset.AudioSetCommands.command_audioset_autoplay_playlist
documentation:
Set a playlist to auto-play songs from.

**Usage**:
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]audioset autoplay playlist playlist_name_OR_id [args]`

**Args**:
​ ​ ​ ​ The following are all optional:
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --scope <scope>
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --author [user]
​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ --guild [guild] **Only the bot owner can use this**

**Scope** is one of the following:
    ​Global
​ ​ ​ ​ Guild
​ ​ ​ ​ User

**Author** can be one of the following:
​ ​ ​ ​ User ID
​ ​ ​ ​ User Mention
​ ​ ​ ​ User Name#123

**Guild** can be one of the following:
​ ​ ​ ​ Guild ID
​ ​ ​ ​ Exact guild name

Example use:
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]audioset autoplay playlist MyGuildPlaylist`
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]audioset autoplay playlist MyGlobalPlaylist --scope Global`
​ ​ ​ ​ `[p]audioset autoplay playlist PersonalPlaylist --scope User --author Draper`

methods.name: redbot.cogs.permissions.permissions.Permissions.__permissions_hook
documentation:
Purpose of this hook is to prevent guild owner lockouts of permissions specifically
without modifying rule behavior in any other case.

Guild owner is not special cased outside of these configuration commands
to allow guild owner to restrict the use of potentially damaging commands
such as, but not limited to, cleanup to specific channels.

Leaving the configuration commands special cased allows guild owners to fix
any misconfigurations.

classes.name: redbot.logging.RotatingFileHandler
documentation:
Custom rotating file handler.

This file handler rotates a bit differently to the one in stdlib.

For a start, this works off of a "stem" and a "directory". The stem
is the base name of the log file, without the extension. The
directory is where all log files (including backups) will be placed.

Secondly, this logger rotates files downwards, and new logs are
*started* with the backup number incremented. The stdlib handler
rotates files upwards, and this leaves the logs in reverse order.

Thirdly, naming conventions are not customisable with this class.
Logs will initially be named in the format "{stem}.log", and after
rotating, the first log file will be renamed "{stem}-part1.log",
and a new file "{stem}-part2.log" will be created for logging to
continue.

A few things can't be modified in this handler: it must use append
mode, it doesn't support use of the `delay` arg, and it will ignore
custom namers and rotators.

When this handler is instantiated, it will search through the
directory for logs from previous runtimes, and will open the file
with the highest backup number to append to.

classes.name: redbot.core.errors.CannotSetSubfield
documentation:
Tried to set sub-field of an invalid data structure.

This would occur in the following example:

```
>>> import asyncio
>>> from redbot.core import Config
>>> config = Config.get_conf(None, 1234, cog_name="Example")
>>> async def example():
...     await config.foo.set(True)
...     await config.set_raw("foo", "bar", False)  # Should raise here
...
>>> asyncio.run(example())
```

functions.name: redbot.__main__.run_bot
documentation:
This runs the bot.

Any shutdown which is a result of not being able to log in needs to raise
a SystemExit exception.

If the bot starts normally, the bot should be left to handle the exit case.
It will raise SystemExit in a task, which will reach the event loop and
interrupt running forever, then trigger our cleanup process, and does not
need additional handling in this function.

methods.name: redbot.core._rpc.RPCMixin.register_rpc_handler
documentation:
Registers a method to act as an RPC handler if the internal RPC server is active.

When calling this method through the RPC server, use the naming scheme
"cogname\_\_methodname".

:::{important}
All parameters to RPC handler methods must be JSON serializable objects.
The return value of handler methods must also be JSON serializable.
:::

:::{important}
RPC support is included in Red on a `provisional <developer-guarantees-exclusions>` basis.
Backwards incompatible changes (up to and including removal of the RPC) may occur if deemed necessary.
:::

# Parameters

method

: The method to register with the internal RPC server.

methods.name: redbot.core._rpc.RPCMixin.unregister_rpc_handler
documentation:
Deregisters an RPC method handler.

This will be called automatically for you on cog unload and will pass silently if the
method is not previously registered.

:::{important}
RPC support is included in Red on a `provisional <developer-guarantees-exclusions>` basis.
Backwards incompatible changes (up to and including removal of the RPC) may occur if deemed necessary.
:::

# Parameters

method

: The method to unregister from the internal RPC server.

functions.name: redbot.core.modlog.create_case
documentation:
Creates a new case.

This fires an event {code}`on_modlog_case_create`

# Parameters

bot: Red

: The bot object

guild: discord.Guild

: The guild the action was taken in

created_at: datetime

: The time the action occurred at.
  If naive `datetime` object is passed, it's treated as a local time
  (similarly to how Python treats naive `datetime` objects).

action_type: str

: The type of action that was taken

user: Union[discord.Object, discord.abc.User, int]

: The user target by the action

moderator: Optional\[Union[discord.Object, discord.abc.User, int]\]

: The moderator who took the action

reason: Optional[str]

: The reason the action was taken

until: Optional[datetime]

: The time the action is in effect until.
  If naive `datetime` object is passed, it's treated as a local time
  (similarly to how Python treats naive `datetime` objects).

channel: Optional\[Union[discord.abc.GuildChannel, discord.Thread]\]

: The channel the action was taken in

last_known_username: Optional[str]

: The last known user handle (`username` / `username#1234`) of the user
  Note: This is ignored if a Member or User object is provided
  in the user field

# Raises

ValueError

: If the action type is not a valid action type.

RuntimeError

: If user is the bot itself.

TypeError

: If `channel` is of type `discord.PartialMessageable`.
